title: centaurea × javorkae
intro: Centaurea × javorkae, also known as Javorka's knapweed, is a hybrid species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, derived from the cross between Centaurea jankae and Centaurea phrygia. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Centaurea × javorkae include its potential to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, as well as its ability to act as an antiseptic and disinfectant. Traditionally, herbal preparations of Centaurea × javorkae have been used in folk medicine, often in the form of infusions, decoctions, or topical applications, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
